data.title: Intelligent Living regains Nasdaq minimum bid price compliance
data.description:
• Intelligent Living (NASDAQ:ILAG) on Thursday regained Nasdaq listing compliance after meeting the $1 minimum bid price rule.
  • The company said on Jan. 14, 2026, it regained compliance with Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid price requirement after its shares closed above $1 for 10 consecutive trading days.
  • Shares +2.62%.

data.title: enVVeno Medical announces reverse stock split; shares down 20%
data.description:
• enVVeno Medical (NVNO) announced on Thursday that the company's board of directors has approved a reverse stock split of its shares of common stock at a ratio of 1 for 35.
  • The reverse stock split will become effective at 12:01 a.m. Eastern time on January 20, 2026, and the company's common stock will open for trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on a post-split basis on January 20, 2026, under the company's existing trading symbol "NVNO". 
  • At the effective time of the reverse stock split, every 35 shares of NVNO common stock issued and outstanding will be combined into one share of common stock issued and outstanding. This will reduce the company's outstanding common stock from approximately 22.9 million shares to approximately 655 thousand shares.
  • NVNO -19.69% premarket to $0.2761.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: F5 climbs after seeing bullish upgrade at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
J.P. Morgan upgraded shares of F5 (FFIV) to Overweight from Neutral and raised the price target on the stock to $345 from $295.

Shares of F5 — which provides networking and communications equipment and software solutions — rose about 3% premarket on Thursday.

"We are upgrading shares of FFIV to Overweight from Neutral and raising our PT to $345 from $295 as we believe the risks contemplated to the financial outlook for FY26 on account of the security incident were overly conservative and we see upside to both consensus earnings estimates medium term as well as upside in relation to the valuation multiple which has retraced since the time of disclosure of the security incident," said analysts led by Samik Chatterjee.

The analysts noted that vital to their bullish thesis on F5 shares is recent distributor feedback on robust execution and post-incident response from the company, including offering Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor and OverWatch Threat Hunting to F5 BIG-IP customers. This has also driven customer activity and engagement to remain largely robust even through this phase of disruption, according to the analysts.

"We expect concerns around a slowdown in customer activity with F5 to be overblown, with the headwinds in our view largely confined to a quarter (F1Q), following which we expect the business to return to a normal pace of activity," said Chatterjee and his team.

data.title: NNN REIT declares $0.60 dividend
data.description:
• NNN REIT (NNN) declares $0.60/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 5.72%
  • Payable Feb. 13; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • NNN is one of only three publicly traded REITs to have increased its annual dividend for 36 or more consecutive years.
  • See NNN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Jabil prices $1 billion bond offering in two tranches
data.description:
• Jabil (JBL) (NYSE: JBL) said on Thursday it has priced a $1 billion debt offering, consisting of $500 million of 4.200% senior notes due 2029 and $500 million of 4.750% senior notes due 2033.
  • The electronics manufacturing company said the offering is expected to close on January 23, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • Jabil plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including repayment of its $500 million 1.700% senior notes due 2026 at or before maturity.
  • JBL +1.01% premarket to $243.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Initial jobless claims drop more than expected in past week
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for the week ended Jan. 10: -9K to 198K vs. 215K consensus and 207K prior (revised from +208K), according to data released by the Department of Labor on Thursday.

Continuing claims for the week ended Jan. 3: 1.884M vs. 1.890M consensus and 1.903M prior (revised from 1.914M).

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: U.S. pushes allied pact to cut reliance on Chinese minerals
data.description:
The United States plans to bring together foreign ministers from allied nations next month to advance an agreement aimed at reducing dependence on Chinese critical minerals, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the plans.

The meeting, expected around Feb. 4 and hosted by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will focus on strengthening and diversifying supply chains for minerals seen as essential to advanced manufacturing and clean energy.

Washington has made lowering reliance on China a priority since Beijing imposed export limits on certain rare earth materials last year. While those restrictions were temporarily eased following talks between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping, U.S. officials say momentum has increased to secure longer-term alternatives.

The U.S. has urged some European Union members to sign bilateral agreements, though the European Commission has encouraged a coordinated EU-wide approach. Diplomats said negotiations have been complicated by uncertainty over leadership, with Rubio, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Trade Representative Jamieson Greer all involved.

Greer said the goal is to build resilient and economically viable mineral markets with partner countries. Officials had hoped to reach initial agreements by late January, but that timeline now appears unlikely, according to the report.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Q4 earnings, revenue top, helped by investment banking, wealth management
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (MS) Q4 earnings beat the average analyst estimate, helped by solid revenue growth from its wealth management and investment management units, both from the prior and year-ago quarters. In institutional securities, investment banking revenue jumped 47% Y/Y.

Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.68, topping the average analyst estimate of $2.42, fell from $2.80 in Q3 and rose from $2.22 in Q4 2024.

Net revenue of $17.9B, exceeding the $17.8B consensus, dropped from $18.2B in the prior quarter and grew from $16.2B a year ago.

Morgan Stanley’s (MS) net interest income of $2.86B, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.53B, increased from $2.49B in Q3 and $2.55B in the prior year’s Q4.

Pretax profit margin of 32% vs. 33% in the previous quarter and 30% a year ago. Compensation and benefits, as a percentage of net revenue, were 39%, compared with 41% in Q3 and 39% in the year-ago Q4.

Total non-compensation expenses were $5.05B vs. $4.75B in the prior quarter and $4.91B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $18M, vs. $88.3M Visible Alpha consensus, rose from $0M in the previous quarter and fell from $115M a year ago.

Return on average tangible common equity of 21.8% vs. 23.5% in Q3 and 20.2% in Q4 2024.

Morgan Stanley (MS) stock edged up 0.1% in Thursday premarket trading.

Revenue by segment:

  • Institutional Securities: $7.93B dipped 7% Q/Q and increased 9% Y/Y; investment banking revenue of $2.41B increased 14% Q/Q and 47% Y/Y. Equity net revenue of $3.67B dropped 11% Q/Q and rose 10% Y/Y, and fixed income net revenue of $1.76B fell 19% Q/Q and 9% Y/Y.

  • Wealth Management: $8.43B, up 2% Q/Q and 13% Y/Y. Asset management revenue of $5.03B climbed 5% Q/Q and 14% Y/Y. Total client assets of $7.38T grew 5% Q/Q and 19% Y/Y.

  • Investment Management: $1.72B grew 4% Q/Q and 5% Y/Y. Asset management and related fees of $1.65B rose 7% Q/Q and 6% Y/Y. Long-term net inflows were $1.7B in Q4 2025, down from $16.5B in Q3 and $4.3B in Q4 2024. Long-term assets under management or supervision of $1.25T rose 1% Q/Q and 14% Y/Y.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Morgan Stanley GAAP EPS of $2.68 beats by $0.23, revenue of $17.89B beats by $140M
